the former Soviet Union.[[108: David Biale et al., Hasidism: A New History (Princeton University Press, 2018), 595; Eliezrie, The Secret of Chabad, chap. 10.]]
This fulfilled the prediction of the Rebbe Rashab that the Rayatz recorded in his diary in 1926:[[109: Sefer HaSichos, 5680–5687, 2nd edition (Brooklyn, NY: Kehot Publication Society, 2004), 132.]] There would be a period of “dark clouds,” but Judaism would survive. “Three weeks before his passing, my father called me into his study and said, ‘There will be difficult times; you will need to have mesirat nefesh bepoel (actual self-sacrifice). But eventually, the Communist government will disintegrate.’”[[110: See chap. 3 of this book.]]
